fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Sport Court; Landscaped Area; Front Driveway; Circular Driveway
fr.considerations: Multiple Structures; Dense Vegetation; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response on the property could involve refining the access routes and visibility of pathways around the multiple structures present. As there is a single access point, ensuring it remains clear and unobstructed by vehicles in both the front and circular driveways will facilitate swift entry and exit for responders. Managing the dense vegetation, particularly around access routes and open areas like the sport court, may assist in improving visibility and movement, allowing emergency personnel to navigate efficiently between structures. Clearly marked and maintained pathways from the access point to key areas, such as buildings and the landscaped regions, could also support effective staging and coordinated response efforts.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Trees; Dense Shrub Cover; Scattered Shrubs; Open Green Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Access Route; Defensible Space; Open Terrain; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between dense shrub cover and the structure could improve wildfire resilience. Managing vegetation adjacent to the structure, such as trimming back shrubs and maintaining open terrain, could reduce potential fire exposure. Preserving open green spaces and maintaining them free of excess debris can act as fuel breaks, helping to reduce fuel continuity. Additionally, reducing the density of scattered trees and shrubs around the property may limit potential pathways for fire to travel. Secure and maintain the vegetated access route to support firefighter access and facilitate suppression efforts. These actions could collectively support long-term vegetation management and improve overall property preparedness.
